rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $763.00 U.S.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories [Proposed Order]
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 17, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner's First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner's First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories [Proposed Order]. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The People of the State of California's (the "People") Motion to Compel Responses to its Demand for Production of Documents, Special Interrogatories, and Form Interrogatories is GRANTED. Since the People failed to notice, failed to state in the caption, failed to persuasively argue in memorandum, and failed to set out support in declaration in support of sanctions, sanctions of any and all sort are DENIED. All parties to appear. Assistant District Attorney Pamela Pecora-Hansen shall be prepared to represent to the Court that she did in fact sign or authorized the signing of the moving papers and did sign or authorize the signing of her declaration. Although, technically, failing to sign papers in support of a discovery motion is not a violation of the California Code of Civil Procedure, see Cal. Civ. Proc. Code s 128.7(g), failing to sign moving papers is disfavored, and failing to personally sign a declaration throws the contents of the declaration into question. In the event it is found that counsel did not personally sign her declaration, counsel shall provide authority as to how this Court can admit the declaration signed on her behalf by a third party. In the alternative, counsel shall file and serve a validly-signed declaration. In the absence of a satisfactory resolution of this issue, the People's Motion is DENIED. Using contact information available, the People shall notify Claimant Paul Issel ("Issel") of this tentative and of the date, time, and location of the instant hearing. At hearing, the People shall submit a proposed order setting out the relief it seeks, describing efforts made to notify Mr. Issel of this hearing, and integrating the contents of this tentative ruling. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION $764.00 U.S
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 17, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA's MOTION TO STRIKE The motion to strike is granted, with leave for claimant to file an amended Claim Opposing Forfeiture within 15 days of notice of entry of this order. Claimant filed a "Claim Opposing Forfeiture" form. On box 5, he has not specified the nature of his "interest in the claimed property," other than to identify the property itself as "cash currency and fanny pack." If Claimant contends that he is the "owner" of the property, he must check box 5(a), or otherwise explain in paragraph 5 what is his "interest in the claimed property." The moving party shall serve a blank copy of Judicial Council Form MC 200 with this Order. No opposition was filed to this motion to strike. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARIA BUZON et al VS. ALEXANDER ROBERT VARTAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Discovery; Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 17, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT ALEXANDER VARTAN Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Douglas Robbins, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Alexander Robert Vartan's ("Vartan") Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery and for Monetary Sanctions is DENIED without prejudice. Vartan's request for sanctions is DENIED without prejudice. Since Plaintiffs Rosa Maria Buzon and Lee Nathan Buzon (collectively the "Buzons") failed to oppose the Motion, sanctions in favor of the prevailing party is DENIED. "A motion filed without the supporting papers upon which it is based is treated as an 'incomplete motion' and may be continued, placed off calendar or denied without prejudice." Robert I Weil, et al., Civil Procedure Before Trial s 9:28.1 (The Rutter Group 2014 ed.); see IIIB Bay Area Local Court Rules: KeyRules, at 875 (2013 ed.) (citing Weil and Brown). Here Vartan claims in moving papers to have attached the discovery at issue to declaration at "Exhibit A." While a proof of service can be found at Exhibit A, the discovery requests themselves are missing. Without a review of the discovery requests, the Court cannot find "good cause" to authorize an order to compel. Using contact information available, Vartan shall notify the Buzons of this tentative and of the date, time, and location of the instant hearing. Although the Buzons are the prevailing party, Vartan (not the Buzons) shall prepare a form of the order consistent with this tentative ruling and shall bring a copy of the proposed order to the hearing, if any. In the absence of a hearing, Vartan shall timely comply with Cal. R. Ct. 3.1312 as if Vartan was the prevailing party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to drobbins@woodrobbins.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge.
